like a ritual I can’t unlearn.
The key still fits,
but the lock feels different now.
I don’t turn it.
Just hold it there,
warm in my palm,
like a secret I’m not ready to tell.
The house breathes without you.

The clock hands don’t move,
just hum,
like a fridge in an empty kitchen.
Some nights
they forget to tick,
and the dark gets heavier,
not from absence,
but from the weight of what’s unsaid.
The walls remember every argument,
every laugh,
every door left unopened.
They breathe,
slow and steady,
until you learn to listen.

scripts.topic: I Didn't Know Love Could Feel This Soft
scripts.script:
There's a tenderness that hums,
like the fridge at 3 a.m.
No one hears it,
but it keeps everything fresh.
Your touch,
a quiet current
pulling the sheets into place.
I didn't know bodies could settle
like dust after rain,
soft,
unnoticed,
perfectly there.
send this to them.

Lao Tzu whispered
not the loudest voice
but the one that listens
earns the mountain's trust.

you’ve spent years
building altars to others’ names,
while your own name
faded in the wind.

what if the revolution
isn’t a shout
but the moment
you stop apologizing
for taking up space.
visual_keywords: single lantern in a dark room
hands cupped around a candle flame

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
You’re tracing the cracks in the ceiling again.
A spider weaves its web,
quiet architect of something delicate.
You call it luck.
I call it proof.
But the voice hums like static,
steady as the fridge light,
buzzing you’re not built to last.
The web trembles,
still holds.
You exhale.
The cracks don’t.
send this to yourself.

YOU TURNED THE PAGE
too fast
I didn’t finish reading
but the ink
bled through anyway
like a secret I wasn’t supposed to see
YOU SMILED
and the room tilted
just enough to notice
the way your shadow
stretched longer than mine
I knew then
we were standing in different light

scripts.topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
scripts.script:
The porch light stays on all night.
I didn’t ask it to.
but it flickers,
like a pulse I can’t control.
Some doors,
you don’t get to close,
they stay ajar,
letting in the cold you can’t stop.
The key,
though,
is still yours—
what you lock,
what you leave open,
what you walk away from.
Turn it slow.

You're tracing circles on my palm.
Like you're memorizing the shape of my bones,
not the skin.
I don’t flinch.
Even when your finger lingers too long,
I let the quiet weight of it
press into me—
not a promise,
just proof.
You don’t have to say it.
The way you stay
is enough.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I forget my name
when you walk in
just
breathe
and the room tilts
soft as a hammock in summer.
WE DON’T TOUCH
WE DON’T SPEAK
but the air hums
like a porch swing
creaking in the dark.
Your sleeve brushes mine
and suddenly
I remember how to stay.

The room was always white,
but you brought the rain.
Not the storm,
just the kind that blurs the window,
makes the world outside feel softer.
I stopped counting days,
started measuring time in droplets.
Somewhere between the first drop
and the last,
the walls learned to breathe.
Now the air hums,
not empty,
just different.

Hafiz knew this truth—
they will take what you lend
but never what you keep.

watch how the moon
never apologizes
for its light,
yet the night still holds it.

you are not a well
you are the sky
that decides what rain to catch.

some hands cup only thirst,
some hands cup only stars—
choose which one you feed.

scripts.topic: The Quiet Kind of Love Nobody Talks About
scripts.script:
You kept the light on
in the hallway
for years
I never asked
why
until the night
you forgot
and I stood there
counting shadows
like unopened letters
wondering
if you knew
I was home.

I waited
by the screen door
until the hinge creaked
its tired song
YOU NEVER SHOWED
not even a shadow
stretching down the hall
to say I’m sorry
I kept the light on
all summer
just in case
you changed your mind
still warm
in the dark

I stopped counting the steps.
The staircase creaks only for me,
and the echo learns my name.
Sometimes I leave the door unlocked,
not for company,
but so the wind can visit.
It brings the scent of rain on pavement,
slowly teaching me the shape of empty rooms.
I trace the cracks in the ceiling,
pretending they’re constellations,
and I’m the only one who knows their stories.
The quiet isn’t empty.
It’s a bowl I’m learning to hold.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that has no mouth.
It gnaws at the edges of your ribs,
not for food,
but for the way light bends through leaves.
EXISTING is counting breaths,
LIVING is tasting the air after rain.
The clock hands don't move,
they dissolve into the hum of the fridge.
You don't notice the difference
until you catch yourself smiling at nothing.
The world tilts,
just enough to let the gold spill in.
Send this to them.

Shadows crept in
we just
stopped believing
in forever promises
and somewhere between then and NOW
we lost our grip
on the pieces of us
still trying to
find our way back
SLOWLY
we unraveled
and the ache remains

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the empty chairs,
The table set for two,
just me,
and the ghost of your excuses.
Some nights,
I still hear the clink of your fork,
but the plate is cold,
The candle never lit.
I won’t ask again,
The door stays open,
not for you,
but for the wind to carry the dust away.

There's a quiet like the first page of a notebook,
empty but already humming.
The pen hovers,
waiting for the courage to press down.
Some roads don't need signs,
they just know the way your shoes fit.
Trust isn't a sudden yes,
but the slow unclenching of your grip.
The map was never the journey,
only your hands tracing the edges.
Let the ink bleed,
let the pages fill.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Friends Who Only Call When They Need You
scripts.script:
There's a kind of ache
that wears your name like borrowed shoes.
They only fit
when the road gets rough,
soft soles padding their way to you.
The laces come undone,
and suddenly,
you're standing barefoot in the hallway,
watching them walk out
with the rain still on their jacket.
The door clicks shut.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Beauty of an Unfinished Life
scripts.script:
the old gardener whispered
some seeds never bloom
some roots twist underground
forever reaching
never breaking soil.

you call it failure
i call it quiet growth
what if the unfinished
is the only art we truly make
what if the cracks
are the light getting in
not the end
but the shape of becoming

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
a stranger once whispered
across the crowded room
the moon doesn’t ask the tide
to stay
yet it pulls anyway.

you trace their name
in steam on the bathroom mirror
knowing it will vanish
before the water cools.

what is heavier
a feather held in the hand
or one carried
for miles in the wind.

let the weight be yours alone
no need to name it.

the old gardener whispered
some roots tangle only for a season.
some branches bend
till the wind decides to let them rise.
you will carry the shape of their weight
long after the storm has passed.
but soil remembers every seed
that chose not to stay.
you are not the garden
you are the rain
that taught it how to grow without them.
let the empty rows
remind you what blooms next.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
How do you name the moment
when the light catches your eyelashes
and you don’t flinch—
just stare through the window
like the world outside is a story
you’ve already read,
but keep turning pages anyway.
I trace the shape of your shoulders,
the way they curve like a question
no one’s brave enough to ask.
You don’t know I’m here,
and that’s the only time
you look like home.

scripts.topic: When Healing Hurts More Than the Wound
scripts.script:
The scar tissue is thicker,
stretching,
pulling,
like a sail in the wrong wind.
It catches every gust,
but never lifts.
Some mornings,
the ache isn’t the cut—
it’s the stitches,
tugging,
reminding,
this is how you mend.

You're folding the blanket just so.
The way it tucks under your chin
like a promise no one else remembers.
I watch the corners align,
perfect creases holding the dark.
This is the safest place I know —
not a room,
not a bed,
but the exact second
your hands pause mid-air,
breathing me in without looking up.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
4:17 p.m. The blinds are half-drawn.
Your eyes
are a compass I can’t calibrate,
pointing everywhere
but never true north.
I’ve memorized the tilt,
the way your lashes cast shadows
like questions I’m too afraid to answer.
Some afternoons,
I let myself believe
you’re searching for something in me.
But the truth is quieter—
just two people
standing in the same light,
seeing completely different skies.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that has no plate,
no fork,
just the shape of your absence
in the chair across from me.
The coffee still steams,
but the cup feels lighter,
like you took a sip
without touching it.
Some spaces don't need distance
to ache,
just the quiet weight
of wanting what's already here.
Send this to them.

You don’t resist. That’s the secret.
The river doesn’t beg the stone to smooth,
just flows
around the edges you thought were sharp.
Some things soften only when you stop trying,
like a door that swings open
when your hands aren’t clenched.
The quietest power is knowing
what to leave untouched,
what to let settle
like dust in sunlight.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
scripts.script:
You’re tracing the crack in the ceiling again.
Like a river you refuse to dam.
I brought no tools,
just the quiet weight of my palm
against your ribs.
You called it surrender.
I called it choosing to stay.
The house still creaks,
the pipes still hum,
but the silence between us
isn’t empty—
it’s full of all the things
we never had to fix.
send this to them.

Some nights,
I trace the cracks in the ceiling,
like rivers on a dry tongue.
They don’t lead anywhere,
just split,
and split again,
until the dark is a delta of questions.
The bed’s too wide,
but I don’t move,
just let the weight of absence
press me into the shape of a comma,
waiting for a sentence that never ends.

It’s not trust. Not like that.
Your name fits in my palm
like a pebble I found by the river,
smooth and unremarkable,
but I keep it anyway.
The way you laugh at nothing
is the only sound I want to memorize,
not for the melody,
but for the way it settles in my ribs
like the first light through curtains,
soft and unasked for.
I don’t need you to stay.
I just need you to exist
somewhere near.
send this to them.

Lao Tzu whispered
let go of the branch
and the wind will carry you
not to fall
but to fly.

I used to grip every leaf
afraid the sky would forget my name.
Now I watch the clouds rearrange themselves
without my permission.
It’s not surrender
it’s the first real breath
after years of holding.

The horizon doesn’t ask for tickets.
visual_keywords: untied boat on a still lake

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
I carry the eggshells in my pockets.
They weigh nothing,
but the cracks sing every time I move.
I laugh,
and the sound is a porcelain bell
no one hears shatter inside.
THEY ASK HOW I STAY SO STRONG,
and I smile,
my teeth the last unbroken pieces.
The basket never empties,
just fills with lighter,
more fragile things.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: How to Carry Loss Without Being Crushed
scripts.script:
YOU LEARN TO FOLD IT
like a letter
kept in your back pocket
not to read
just to know it’s there
THE WEIGHT SHIFTS
with every step
each crease a little softer
until one day
it fits
inside the seam
where no one sees
and you forget
to check.

I stopped checking the clock at 3:17,
just the way shadows do when the sun gives up.
The porch light stayed on,
but the moths found brighter things.
I never told you
I counted their wings,
or how the quiet hum of the fridge
became my favorite lullaby.
Some nights,
the dark tastes like old tea—
bitter, but familiar.
I won’t pour it out.
The house still breathes,
but the air doesn’t miss you anymore.

There's a kind of weight
that doesn't show on scales,
just settles in your shoulders
like a coat you borrowed
for someone else's winter.
The sleeves are too long,
but you keep pulling them down,
covering their hands,
tucking them into pockets
that were never yours.
Some burdens feel like kindness
until your arms forget how to rest.
Let them carry their own cold.

I stopped counting the mornings
when your name tastes like fresh coffee.
The way you stir the sugar in,
slowly...
not too fast,
not too slow,
just enough to make it sweet.
I never told you
I memorized the rhythm of your spoon.
The clink against porcelain,
a lullaby for days I didn’t think I’d keep.
You are the quiet hum
before the world wakes up.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
We are gardeners of our own ghosts,
pulling weeds,
not to erase,
but to make room.
Some days,
the soil resists,
hard and unyielding,
but the hands that dig
know the worth of stubborn roots.
We choose what blooms here,
not by force,
but by the quiet persistence
of light through cracks.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the miles between us,
not because they don’t matter,
but because you’re already here,
carried in the weight of my pockets—
coins, a ticket stub, your laugh folded small.
Some days I pull it out,
just to feel the edges,
not to unfold,
not to explain,
just to know it’s real.
I won’t ask for more,
this quiet gravity is enough.
let them know.

You were a guest
in your own skin
for years
until the door
finally clicked shut behind you.
NO ONE TOLD YOU
it was okay
to stay.
You learned the shape
of your own shadow
then stepped into it
like a room
with the lights already on.
visual_keywords: the quiet hum of a refrigerator

scripts.script:
You’re folding the same shirt again.
Third time tonight.
The creases remember your hands,
but...
the fabric doesn’t.
Some changes are invisible
until the light hits them just right.
You used to iron at dawn,
now...
even the wrinkles feel like proof.
Not of time,
of you.
Staying.
Different.

The first time I knew wasn’t a moment.
It was a slow unraveling,
like a sweater pulled loose at the seam.
not from force,
just time and gravity,
thread by thread,
until the whole thing sagged,
and I stood there,
holding the weight of what used to fit,
wondering when it stopped,
but knowing,
I’d never wear it the same way again.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
The first light through the blinds,
not dawn,
just the streetlamp giving up.
I count the cracks in the ceiling,
like Morse code from another life.
Some nights,
the sheets still smell like your detergent,
not memory,
something real.
The clock ticks,
but it’s not time,
just habit wearing thin.
I reach for my phone,
not to call,
just to see your name,
still there,
still first.

Lao Tzu whispered
not walls or a roof
the weight of a bowl in your hands
warm broth rising
steam curling like a question you forgot to ask.

you’ve carried that bowl before
the way your fingers remember the curve
the way your breath steadies
before the first sip.

home is the pause
the unclenching
before the world rushes back in.
visual_keywords: steam rising from a clay bowl

scripts.script:
It’s not goodbye. It’s just geography.
The way the tide leaves shells,
then returns to claim them,
without apology.
You’ll find one in your pocket,
months from now,
still warm from the sun.
It doesn’t belong to you,
but you’ll keep it anyway.
The ocean doesn’t ask for souvenirs.
Neither did I.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
THE WAY YOUR LAUGHTER
starts
before the joke lands.
You tilt your head
just a little
when you’re listening.
I COUNT THE FRECKLES
on your left wrist
like constellations.
You hum
old songs under your breath
while tying your shoes.
I KNOW THE SHAPE
of your shadow
when the sun hits it right.
You leave crumbs
on the counter
like tiny breadcrumbs back.

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
IT WHISPERS
while you tie your shoes
it’s the second guess
before the door clicks shut
YOU COULD STAY
just one more minute
one more rehearsal
one more coat of paint
it hums
beneath the applause
the handshake
the thank-you note
YOU SHOULD HAVE
said more
done less
been different
it lingers
like static
on a dead channel
waiting
for the next commercial break.

THE TEARS COME
like summer rain on a screen door
no one opened
no one asked for
just
steady
steady
I press my palm to the glass
fog blooms
around the shape of my hand
and for a second
everything
is softer
then it’s gone.

You are the hush
before the storm
I never saw coming.
I held my breath
and you were water
seeping through my fingers,
cool,
unhurried.
THE SKY REMEMBERS
how we used to lie there,
counting clouds
that dissolved
into nothing.

I stopped waiting for the door to creak open,
just stood there,
keys in my own palm,
The lock turned,
not with a click,
but with the weight of knowing,
Some thresholds aren’t meant to be crossed twice,
The hallway stretches,
empty and gold,
and I walk,
not toward,
not away,
just home.

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
You’re rearranging the same three mugs,
again.
The one with the chip,
still your favorite,
just sits there,
collecting dust like a museum piece.
Some mornings,
you pour the coffee,
but the cup stays empty,
waiting for a hand that never reaches.
You laugh,
but the kettle’s whistle is hollow,
and the steam,
just fogs the window.
You were the backup plan,
the extra setting at the table,
quietly,
polishing the silverware.

scripts.topic: When You Are the One Who Always Forgives
scripts.script:
YOU SWALLOW THE WORDS
before they sting
like bitter pills
meant to heal.
I watch
the way your jaw unclenches
slowly
like a door left ajar
for the hundredth time.
The air doesn’t change
but it should.
You always let me back in
while I pretend
not to notice
you’re already halfway gone.

The porch light stays on all night.
Only for you.
A single bulb,
softly humming your name.
I trace the cracks in the sidewalk,
counting steps to your door.
The neighbor’s dog still wags,
but only when your shadow passes.
Even the mailbox waits,
letters addressed to both of us.
I leave the key under the mat,
knowing you’ll never use it.
The house exhales when you’re near.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being Tired in Ways Sleep Cannot Fix
scripts.script:
4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
but the steam won’t warm this ache.
I’ve counted every crack in the ceiling,
Slowly,
they’ve become constellations of my fatigue.
Some nights,
the weight isn’t in my bones—
It’s in the air,
thick like syrup,
and I’m drowning,
not in water,
but in the quiet drag of minutes.
The clock’s hands,
steady and cruel,
know this tired isn’t sleep’s to fix.

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
I am a shadow
stretching in the wrong direction,
leaning toward light I can’t touch.
Some days,
it feels like gravity,
this pull I didn’t ask for.
The way my name sounds
when it leaves your lips—
a question you’ll never answer.
I trace the edges of your absence,
like a blind man memorizing a face.
Not to keep,
just to know.

A traveler once whispered
across the dunes of time
words are footprints
left in the sand
before the tide returns.

you trace them again
fingers hovering
like clouds over a forgotten shore
wondering whose steps they were
or if they were ever yours at all.

the wind doesn’t answer
it only carries.

scripts.topic: Loving Someone Who Can't Love Back
scripts.script:
You’re watering a plant that won’t grow.
The soil stays dry,
no matter how gentle your hands.
Some people,
just don’t drink.
You whisper to the leaves,
but the roots never hear.
It’s not cruelty,
just a different kind of air.
You pour anyway,
because hope is a habit.
The pot stays empty.
Let them go.

scripts.topic: I Didn't Know Love Could Feel This Soft
scripts.script:
I thought tenderness was a myth
whispered
by poets
who never held
anything fragile enough.
YOUR HANDS
are the first thing
that ever learned me
without breaking.
I didn’t know
skin could memorize kindness
like a lullaby.
Now every room
feels too loud
without your breath
on my neck.

scripts.topic: Why the Wound Is the Gift
scripts.script:
The cut you can’t see
heals into a door,
swollen at the edges,
just waiting for the right key.
Some nights it creaks open,
revealing not darkness,
but a room full of stars
stitched from your own lost light.
You don’t step inside,
not yet,
just trace the constellations
with trembling fingers,
and realize the wound was never a hole—
it was a telescope.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I remember the weight of your keys
on the table
between us
like something we both pretended not to hear.
You’d reach
and for a second
my shoulder would lean in
not to touch
you
but the space your hand just left.
The radio hummed
old static
and I counted the cracks in the ceiling
until they blurred
into constellations
we never named.

Hafiz whispered
some souls are lanterns
lit from within
not by the wind
but by something older.

you’ll know them
when the room brightens
without a single candle lit,
when the weight of years
feels lighter on your shoulders.

stay for the glow
that doesn’t ask for matches.

scripts.topic: When You Miss Someone You Should Not
scripts.script:
You keep the porch light on for them.
Still warm,
but the bulb’s been dead for weeks.
Some nights,
you swear it flickers,
just enough to trick your chest.
The house knows better,
quietly rearranging its shadows
to make room for what won’t stay.
You press your palm to the window,
not to feel the cold,
but to prove the glass is real.
Send this to them.

A river doesn’t apologize
for carving its own path,
just hums against the banks,
soft but unshaken.
Some days you’ll be the current,
some days the stone,
but never the flood that drowns itself.
The hands that cup the water
aren’t trying to own it,
just hold it long enough
to remember the shape of letting go.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
You tilt your head
just
like the old porch swing
creaking in summer rain.
The way your fingers trace
absent circles on your knee
reminds me
of my grandmother’s quilt
stitched with threads
she never named.
I could watch forever
but you turn
and the spell breaks—
I am caught.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the freckles on your collarbone.
They were constellations I memorized in the dark,
but the sky shifts when you turn over.
Your breath is a tide I can’t predict,
and I’ve learned to float instead of anchor.
Some nights I trace the veins in your wrist,
slowly like a river learning its own name.
I never told you how the light through your lashes
makes me believe in stained glass again.
You are the quietest kind of miracle,
and I keep it folded in my pocket like a secret ticket.
send this to them.

I stopped counting the miles
back to your doorstep
but the scent of rain on pavement
still pulls me home
Not the walls
or the street name
just the way your laugh
sits in the kitchen light
Some nights
I forget the address
and follow the echo instead
quietly
like a compass
made of memory.

When did the weight of your absence
stop crushing my ribs
and start sharpening my edges,
not as punishment
but as a whetstone for living?
I trace the cracks in the sidewalk
where your laughter once split the concrete,
and suddenly,
the world feels less like a place to endure
and more like a lesson I’m finally ready to learn.
Grief isn’t the ghost at the table anymore—
it’s the chair I pull up for myself,
quietly,
to keep the conversation going.

When did the sky stop being blue for you,
not because it changed,
but because you learned to name the clouds differently.
Some mornings,
you wake up,
and the air tastes like old birthday candles,
slowly melting into nothing.
The hands that once held yours,
now forget the shape of your fingers,
and you,
quietly,
let them.
It’s not sadness,
just the sound of a door,
closing on a room you’ll never enter again.

You’re folding the same shirt again.
Third time tonight.
The crease won’t hold,
but neither will you.
Some days,
acceptance isn’t a door you walk through—
it’s the window you stop boarding up.
Light spills in,
not golden,
just ordinary,
and you let it.
The weight you carried wasn’t yours to keep.
Put it down.

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
Gravity pulls harder on some mornings.
The bed resists,
but your voice is the hinge
that lets the light in.
Not with answers,
just the quiet certainty
you’d carry half the weight
if I asked.
Some days,
that’s the only bridge
I need to cross.
You make the hard days easier.
Let them know.

scripts.topic: The Weight of Being the Strong One
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in my spine
from carrying everyone else’s skies
The weight doesn’t bend me anymore
it just settles deeper
like river stones in winter
Some nights I press my palms to the ceiling
and push up
but the roof stays low
I won’t let them see the tremors
only the steady hands holding their world
I built this strength brick by brick
and now I’m the house
no one notices the foundation
until it starts to sink
let them know.
visual_keywords: shadow of a solitary figure under streetlight

scripts.script:
You're tracing the crack in the ceiling.
Like a river deciding its path,
you hesitate at the fork.
The bedsheet still holds the shape of yesterday,
but the pillow smells like fresh laundry now.
SOME DAYS,
you won't need a reason to stand up.
The morning light will find you mid-step,
unsteady but certain.
You'll leave the door unlocked,
just in case courage comes knocking.
Send this to yourself tomorrow.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
Hafiz knew this—

the one you miss
is the one holding your coat
while you stare at the rain.

what you ache for
isn’t absence
it’s the space between your ribs
the echo of a name
you keep swallowing.

you reach for them
like a child groping the dark
for a hand that’s already there—
warm, familiar,
touching your shoulder.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
You check the weather before you leave.
Every single time.
Not for rain,
not for wind,
just to see if it matches the forecast in your head—
The one where you’re both outside,
breathing the same air.
Some days,
the temperature feels like a lie.
It’s not cold.
It’s just missing you.
I won’t ask why you still do this.
Neither will the sky.

The porch light stayed on all summer.
You stopped leaving it off,
not out of kindness,
just…
no one asked why anymore.
The neighbors waved,
and you waved back,
but the bulb burned brighter
than any explanation.
Some doors,
you realize,
only open from the inside.
Let them wonder.

You are the hush between tides,
where the ocean forgets its own name,
just for a moment,
letting the sand decide what stays.
Some nights,
I unclench my jaw,
and the stars slip in,
cool as river stones on my tongue.
The world hums,
but you,
you are the pause,
the breath before the next wave,
where nothing needs to be saved.

You’re holding a dandelion clock in your palm.
The seeds are trembling,
not from wind,
just the weight of being alive.
Some moments refuse to stay,
like light on a closing door.
You try to count the hours,
but they dissolve before your fingers.
This is the gift,
not the loss:
You learn to let go
before the stem snaps.
Let them drift.

scripts.topic: When You Cry in the Car Alone
scripts.script:
It’s not the tears. It’s the rain check you wrote yourself.
The wipers keep time,
but the road doesn’t care.
Your breath fogs the window—
a temporary galaxy
no one else will name.
The radio plays static hymns,
and you hum along,
badly.
No one hears the wrong notes.
You’re not broken.
Just a little too soft for this highway.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
There's a quiet like an empty train station,
waiting for the last departure.
Your suitcase clicks on the platform,
and I realize
I’ve been holding my breath for weeks.
The air tastes like rain that won’t fall,
like promises dissolving into mist.
I don’t ask you to stay,
I just memorize the way your shadow
stretches long across the floor,
reaching for me one last time.
The door closes,
but the echo lingers.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
Your keys still jingle in my pocket.
I reach for them,
but the door I unlock isn’t mine.
Some mornings,
the weight of your name
bends the metal like wet paper.
I answer every call,
not because I’m brave,
but because the silence after
would be louder than the breaking.
The house stands,
even when the beams crack.
send this to them.

The old fisherman knew—
too many tides
pass beneath the same dock.
water forgets the shape of the boat
but the wood remembers the weight.

you stayed
not for the light
but the way it pooled
in the cracks you refused to patch.

some anchors
are just rust holding on.
